CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, original_string VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, original_string) VALUES (1, 'hello world');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, original_string) VALUES (7, 'HELLO WORLD');
SELECT ucase(original_string) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT ucase(original_string) AS upper_string FROM test_strings WHERE original_string LIKE '%á%';
SELECT id, ucase(original_string) AS upper_string FROM test_strings WHERE LENGTH(original_string) < 10;
SELECT (SELECT ucase(original_string) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2) AS upper_2, (SELECT ucase(original_string) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3) AS upper_3;
SELECT s.id, ucase(s.original_string) FROM test_strings s JOIN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE original_string IS NOT NULL) AS sub_query ON s.id = sub_query.id;
SELECT ucase(CONCAT(original_string, ' is converted')) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 4;
SELECT ucase(ucase(original_string)) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 5;
SELECT id, original_string, ucase(original_string) FROM test_strings WHERE original_string != ucase(original_string);
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, original_string) VALUES (8, 'こんにちは');
SELECT ucase(original_string) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 8;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ucase(original_string) FROM test_strings) AS unique_upper;
